Peition To Invalidate The Amendments To Victoria L. Brugnara Revocable Trust Dated May 21, 1998, Removal Of Trustees And For Damages Against Krista M. Grugnara Indivdually And As Trustee, Eric J. Brugnara Individually And As Trustee And Kurt D. Huysentruyt And Does 1 Through 50
Off Calendar May-18-2026 Continued To Aug-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Smoot, in person, for Respondents.

The court and counsel briefly discuss the matter. At the conclusion of discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-17-2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Michael McNaughton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Petrucci. Not Reported.

Peition To Invalidate The Amendments To Victoria L. Brugnara Revocable Trust Dated May 21, 1998, Removal Of Trustees And For Damages Against Krista M. Grugnara Indivdually And As Trustee, Eric J. Brugnara Individually And As Trustee And Kurt D. Huysentruyt And Does 1 Through 50
Off Calendar Dec-17-2025 Continued To May-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Luke Brugnara appeared in pro per.
Paul Smoot appeared for respondents Krista Brugnara and Eric Brugnara.
Krista Brugnara, respondent, was present

The matter was called for hearing on the petition to invalidate amendments to trust, for removal of trustees, and for damages. The Court inquired whether Mr. Brugnara intended to pursue his causes of action against the drafting attorney, Kurt Huysentruyt. Mr. Brugnara stated he did not.

The Court addressed case management and next steps. Mr. Brugnara asserted he had not received certain items of alleged personal property, including photographs of his children and other items of sentimental value, and complained that respondents were withholding those items. Krista Brugnara represented that respondents did not have Mr. Brugnara's personal property and denied that the items were in the residence or otherwise in their possession. Mr. Smoot stated he intends to file a motion for protective order regarding discovery. Mr. Brugnara orally requested appointment of a trustee. The Court declined to appoint a trustee on an oral request and advised that any such request must be made by properly noticed petition. Both sides indicated that mediation would not be productive.

The Court continued the matter to May 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for discovery and further case management.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Peition To Invalidate The Amendments To Victoria L. Brugnara Revocable Trust Dated May 21, 1998, Removal Of Trustees And For Damages Against Krista M. Grugnara Indivdually And As Trustee, Eric J. Brugnara Individually And As Trustee And Kurt D. Huysentruyt And Does 1 Through 50
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Continued To Dec-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Demurrer Of Respondents To Petitioners Amended Petition Of Luke D. Brugnara; Demurrer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Luke Brugnara, petitioner and moving party in pro per.
Paul Smoot for respondents Krista M. Brugnara and Eric J. Brugnara.

The matter was called for hearing on respondents' demurrer to the amended petition of Luke Brugnara.

Mr. Smoot challenged the Court's tentative decision to overrule respondent's demurrer.

Mr. Smoot argued that service of notice was complete upon mailing under Probate Code sections 16061.7 and 1215, and that actual receipt was not required and that respondents had complied with statutory notice service requirements. Mr. Brugnara argued that the trustees failed to exercise reasonable diligence and did not send notice to his last known address of his attorneys, and that service was therefore improper.

After hearing argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling overruling the demurrer.

At Mr. Smoot's request, the Court continued the hearing on Mr. Brugnara's petition set for November 19, 2025, to December 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for Mr. Smoot to file a written objection. Mr. Smoot was directed to file a written objection prior to the next hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
